techniques.technique_details: BlackMatter purchased access to already-compromised networks from initial access brokers on underground forums, targeting organizations with 500-15,000 hosts and revenues of $100M+.
WMI used for remote execution across compromised systems.
Scheduled tasks used to execute the ransomware payload at a specific time across target systems.
BlackMatter payloads are packed with custom packers; configuration stored in encrypted form within the binary.
Security tools terminated using the Windows Restart Manager API before encryption begins.
Credential dumping tools used to harvest domain and local account credentials for lateral movement.
System locale and language checked; execution halted on CIS-region machines.
Network shares, mapped drives, and connected resources enumerated to maximize encryption scope.
RDP used for lateral movement with harvested credentials.
SMB used to propagate the ransomware payload to remote hosts across the network.
Data exfiltrated to actor-controlled infrastructure and cloud storage for double extortion via the BlackMatter leak site.
techniques.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage

techniques.technique_details: HTTPS used for C2 communication; Tor onion addresses used for victim portals.

BlackMatter uses Salsa20 for file content encryption and RSA-1024 for key protection. Partial file encryption (first 1MB) used for speed on large files. Targets Windows, Linux (ESXi), and NAS devices. Active July-November 2021 before announcing shutdown; considered successor to DarkSide.
Volume shadow copies removed; Windows recovery options disabled to prevent victim recovery without payment.
